title: Chassis ADR (Architecture Decision Record) frontmatter
description: Frontmatter for an ADR file. ADRs live under docs/adr/ (or docs/chassis/decisions/) as Markdown with a YAML frontmatter block at the top. The frontmatter validates against this schema; the prose below is free-form. ADR `enforces` entries bind stable rule IDs to this decision; the binding-link gate (Milestone A.5) ensures every diagnostic.ruleId resolves to an ADR whose enforces[].rule matches.

id.description: Stable identifier. Format: ADR-NNNN with 4+ digits (e.g. ADR-0001, ADR-0123). Referenced by diagnostic.violated.convention.

status.description: Lifecycle. A diagnostic binding to a superseded/deprecated ADR emits a `stale-ADR-binding` warning.

rule.description: Stable ruleId that this ADR authoritatively defines (matches diagnostic.ruleId pattern).

description.description: Optional one-line hint of what the rule checks.

enforces.description: Stable rule IDs authoritatively defined by this ADR. The binding-link gate fails on diagnostic.ruleId values that don't resolve to any ADR's enforces[] entry.

applies_to.description: Path globs the decision applies to (e.g. `crates/**`, `schemas/metadata/*.json`). Agents use this to scope ADRs relevant to the file they're editing.

supersedes.description: ADR IDs this decision replaces. Superseded ADRs automatically flip to status: superseded by the `chassis adr validate` tool.

examples.title: Minimal valid ADR (no enforces, no supersedes)

enforces.description: ruleId emitted by a gate must resolve to some ADR's enforces[]
warns on use of *-GENERIC fallback ruleIds
ruleId does not match the canonical pattern

examples.title: Schema versioning policy (replaces ad-hoc version notes)
